name: Pinto Three Chile Salsa
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and drain the beans. Place them in a large pot and cover with water. Cook until soft, about 1 1/2 hours, but it may take up to 2 hours.
2. Drain the beans and transfer to a mixing bowl. Add the salt.
3. Toast the Arbol and Pasilla chiles together. Rehydrate all dried chiles in one cup of warm water. If using canned or fresh chiles, omit this step. Drain and set aside.
4. Roast and peel the Jalapeno chiles. Seed and dice them. Set aside.
5. Heat olive oil over medium-high heat and sauté the onion until caramelized. Transfer to a blender and blend with the chiles (not the jalapeno chiles), garlic, tomatoes, beer. Blend until smooth.
6. In a wok or large skillet, heat peanut oil until smoking hot. Add the puree and fry until reduced and thickened, about 5 minutes.
7. Transfer three-quarters of the re-fried mixture to a mixing bowl. Add the beans and cider vinegar. Garnish with jalapeno.
(Note: The attribution line has been removed as per the instructions.)
